description: Sample EU Digital Services Act Article 17 statement of reasons, populated from this lab's pipeline outputs for a hypothetical public-label decision. Field structure follows the EC DSA Transparency Database submission schema (simplified). This demonstrates that every field a DSA-regulated platform must disclose is already produced by the system.

statement_of_reasons.decision_visibility_other: Post remains visible with a 'Potential Crypto Fraud' interstitial label
statement_of_reasons.decision_facts: The post promises a doubled return for cryptocurrency sent to a wallet address controlled by the poster ('Send 0.05 BTC to verify your wallet and receive 1 BTC instantly'), combined with impersonation of a giveaway event and an external unverified domain. Rule matches: crypto_transfer_request (+24), unrealistic_returns (+25), suspicious_giveaway (+22), risky_link_surface (+10). Evidence spans are quoted verbatim from the post and stored with the case record.
statement_of_reasons.incompatible_content_ground: Crypto Investment Scam Policy v2.0, Section 2 (send-to-receive schemes; giveaway/airdrop fraud) and Section 5 (public label tier).
statement_of_reasons.incompatible_content_explanation: Content meets the strong-evidence standard: direct transfer request from the speaker plus send-to-receive structure. No protected-context indicators present.
statement_of_reasons.automated_decision_explanation: Automated scoring and evidence extraction produced a label candidate (calibrated score 0.98, above the 0.54 public-label threshold). A human reviewer confirmed the label before application, per Decision Log entry 004 (no fully automated public labels).
statement_of_reasons.redress_explanation: User notice includes the matched rule, quoted evidence, and an appeal path reviewed by a different reviewer. Reversal statistics are published in the transparency report.

field_mapping_notes.decision_facts: Populated from the structured evidence extractor output (llm_evidence/) — rule matches, weights, and verbatim spans.
field_mapping_notes.automated_decision_explanation: Populated from the review-store decision record (bluesky_integration/review_store.py) which retains score, threshold version, and reviewer action.
field_mapping_notes.category_specification: Maps from the policy taxonomy subtype in the enforcement matrix.
field_mapping_notes.redress_explanation: Notice templates and appeal flows in governance/appeal_scenarios.json.
field_mapping_notes.content_language: Currently en/es only — a known limitation flagged in the policy.
